content: By integrating the 2025 ICM curiosity mechanism into unsupervised graph anomaly detection, we can reframe anomaly detection as an autonomous agent's intrinsic reward-driven exploration, where the agent receives intrinsic reward for encoding graph substructures that it cannot predict, turning low-confidence prediction errors into an implicit anomaly signal without requiring labeled anomaly data. This reframes contrastive learning's pull of similar nodes and push of dissimilar nodes to align with the curiosity agent's reward for encountering out-of-distribution graph structures.
source_topics: Contrastive learning based unsupervised graph anomaly detection
trigger_topic: Contrastive learning based unsupervised graph anomaly detection
